comment_text.sn22.9:1.5: In similar contexts, anapekkha (“concern”) and abhinandati are regularly oriented towards the past and future respectively. Nanda and its variants straddle the meanings of “take pleasure” and “crave”. Here the prefix abhi- conveys the sense of “facing towards” in the sense “look forward to enjoying”.

root_text.sn22.9:1.4: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītasmiṁ rūpasmiṁ anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn22.9:1.6: paccuppannassa rūpassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn22.9:1.11: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītesu saṅkhāresu anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn22.9:1.13: paccuppannānaṁ saṅkhārānaṁ nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn22.9:1.16: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītasmiṁ viññāṇasmiṁ anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn22.9:1.18: paccuppannassa viññāṇassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn22.9:1.2: “Mendicants, form of the past and future is impermanent,
translation_text.sn22.9:1.4: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple is not concerned with past form,
translation_text.sn22.9:1.5: doesn’t look forward to enjoying future form,
translation_text.sn22.9:1.6: and they practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding present form.
translation_text.sn22.9:1.14: Consciousness of the past and future is impermanent,
translation_text.sn22.9:1.16: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple is not concerned with past consciousness,
translation_text.sn22.9:1.17: doesn’t look forward to enjoying future consciousness,
translation_text.sn22.9:1.18: and they practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding present consciousness.”
